Mechanical to electric converter name

Text: An early bridge between mechanical power and the Fulgur Fangs electric grid. Pulls rotation from shafts and feeds up to 500 kW into the wire network.
Comment: Mechanical to electric converter description
Advanced mechanical to electric converter name

Text: A heavy-duty converter for industrial districts. Draws up to 2000 mechanical power and feeds it into the electric network.
Comment: Advanced mechanical to electric converter description
Electric pole name

Text: The basic support for local transmission lines. Carries electricity for 20 m, only supports a straight pass-through, and loses 10 kW while the line is actively serving consumers.
Comment: Electric pole description
Splitter name

Text: A compact junction for dense electrical layouts. Works over short distance and can branch the network in up to four directions.
Comment: Splitter description
Transmission tower name

Text: A long-distance backbone tower for moving power between districts. Carries electricity for 200 m and loses 30 kW under active load.
Comment: Transmission tower description
Distributor name

Text: A local substation for neighborhood supply. Connects to the wire network, creates a powered service area, and feeds nearby electric buildings.
Comment: Distributor description
Accumulator name

Text: A buffer for the local electric grid. It does not connect by wire directly: instead it works inside a distributor's service area, stores up to 20000 kWh, and supports the district during shortages.
Comment: Accumulator description
Temporary electric consumer name

Text: A technical consumer for validating the electric grid. Useful for quickly checking connectivity, losses, and delivered power in-game.
Comment: Temporary electric consumer description
